nodes.label: AI as first-pass information filter
nodes.grounding_span: I like to a very quick 'AI pass' where I'll go to AI right away

nodes.label: AI solution reliability
nodes.grounding_span: it gave me a solution but that didn't work

nodes.label: AI as independent solution generator
nodes.grounding_span: I'll let it give me a full solution independently

nodes.label: AI task scope manageability
nodes.label_negative: AI task scope too large or complex
nodes.grounding_span: it was failing more specific edits and was going round and round

nodes.label: AI guidance skill as competitive advantage
nodes.grounding_span: if i can guide AI better than others, it's a win

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI to ease the load of processing info for myself

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI's independent solutions
nodes.grounding_span: I've been pleasantly surprised by how much AI can do

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's iterative failures
nodes.grounding_span: it was failing more specific edits and was going round and round

nodes.label: Ambivalence about skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: I struggle with this personally yeah

nodes.label: Acceptance of inevitable AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: There's no avoiding it. I'll be using AI for work no matter what

nodes.label: Iterative verification — test AI output, then correct or research
nodes.grounding_span: If the answer works I'll go with it, but if it seems a bit off or confusing I'll do my own research

nodes.label: Version control discipline — preserve working states before iterating
nodes.grounding_span: Version control is key with AI. Storing a working solution cause it's so finicky
